title: mcplint consumer profile
description: Declarative capability profile for a tool-definition CONSUMER — an LLM-provider tool-use API (Anthropic, OpenAI, Gemini, ...) or an MCP host (Claude Desktop, Cursor, ...). mcplint is provider-agnostic: every consumer, including the MCP spec itself, is described by one of these files. No vendor is privileged in the engine; the generic provider/* rule family reads whichever profile(s) the run targets.

kind.description: synthetic = a computed intersection (e.g. generic-strict). spec-baseline = an MCP spec revision modelled as a profile.

verified.description: true ONLY when every constraint below was read from the consumer's own documentation (cited in `source`). Findings that rely on a verified:false profile are flagged by meta/profile-unverified so a guessed number never reads as gospel.

source.description: URL(s) of the doc the constraints came from. Required when verified=true.

pattern.description: ECMA regex the full tool name must match.

maxCount.description: Max number of tool definitions accepted in one request.

maxTotalBytes.description: Budget on the serialized tool set, if the consumer documents one.

supportedKeywords.description: ALLOWLIST. If present, any JSON-Schema keyword NOT in this list is treated as unsupported. Use for allowlist consumers like Gemini's OpenAPI subset.

unsupportedKeywords.description: DENYLIST of keywords the consumer rejects (error).

unenforcedKeywords.description: Keywords accepted but silently ignored (won't constrain model output) — flagged at info by default, promoted to error when a strict mode rejects them.

strict.description: Extra constraints that apply ONLY when the consumer's strict / structured-output mode is enabled for this target (config: mode='strict').
